question.text: For a REIT to file a rights issue under the Fast Track method, its units must have been listed on any stock exchange for a period of at least how many years?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, for a fast track rights issue, the units should have been listed on any stock exchange for a period of at least 3 years immediately preceding the record date. **The Trap:** The 3-year listing requirement ensures only established REITs can use the faster process. This is the same as the InvIT fast track requirement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Fast track eligibility criteria include: 3-year listing, all units in demat, Rs. 250 crore market cap of public unitholding, 95% complaint redressal, no show-cause notices pending.
